node.DesignTemplate.label: Design Template
node.PMSMSizing.label: PMSM Sizing
node.IPMSizing.label: IPM Sizing
node.BLDCSizing.label: BLDC Sizing
node.PMSMElectromagnetic.label: PMSM Electromagnetic
node.AirgapFluxDensity.label: Airgap Flux Density
node.PMSMEfficiency.label: PMSM Efficiency
node.Gain.description: Multiplies the input by a constant gain
node.Summation.description: Sums two signals (the second port is subtracted when sign=-1)
node.PIDController.description: Parallel PID with filtered derivative (Kd·N·s/(s+N)). Port 0 = error. Port 1 (optional) = u_sat for back-calculation anti-windup.
node.TransferFunction2.description: Second-order rational function H(s) = (b1·s + b0) / (s² + a1·s + a0). Monic denominator (implicit s² coef. = 1).
node.Saturation.description: Limits the output to the range [Min, Max]
node.DCMotorModel.description: Simplified DC motor: electrical + mechanical dynamics
node.Oscilloscope.description: Multi-channel oscilloscope; one input port per signal to display
node.DataLogger.description: Logs the input signal at the chosen sample rate; supports CSV export
node.PhasePortrait.description: Plots one signal against another (X-Y view)
node.SubGraph.description: Groups nodes into a recursive subgraph. Double-click to enter. Visible ports are materialized internally as SubGraphInput/SubGraphOutput nodes. Before generating Scilab, the codegen flattens each SubGraph inline.
node.InverseKinematics.description: Planar 2R inverse kinematics (elbow up). Inputs: target (x, y). Outputs: joint angles (theta1, theta2). The target is saturated to the reachable workspace.
node.FFTAnalyzer.description: Frequency spectrum of the input signal (online FFT, sliding window).
node.HeatmapSink.description: 2D heatmap: visualizes accumulated values over a grid with interpolation.
node.DistributionSink.description: Histogram / statistical distribution of the input signal over a time window.
node.TerminalDisplay.description: Prints the current value to the status console.
node.SubGraphInput.description: Internal input port of a SubGraph. Stub generated automatically on encapsulation.
node.SubGraphOutput.description: Internal output port of a SubGraph. Stub generated automatically on encapsulation.
node.View3DSink.description: 3D viewer of the glTF model assigned to the device. The input signal animates the model's shaft.
node.View3DThermalSink.description: 3D viewer with thermal coloring of the model. Temperature modulates the mesh color.
node.View3DDeformationSink.description: 3D viewer with animated deformation overlay (modal shapes).
node.MaxwellForce.description: Radial Maxwell force in the airgap. Input: flux density B_g(t). Output: distributed force F_r(t).
node.ModalFrequency.description: Natural frequency of the stator (mode n) as a function of geometry and material properties.
node.TolerancePerturbator.description: Stochastic perturbator: adds normal/uniform variation to a nominal parameter for tolerance analysis.
node.JouleLoss.description: Joule (copper) loss of the stator winding. Inputs: torque T (Nm), back-EMF constant Ke. Output: dissipated power P_Cu (W).
node.CoreLoss.description: Core loss (Bertotti, two terms: hysteresis + eddy currents). Inputs: mechanical ω (rad/s), peak B_g.
node.MechanicalLoss.description: Friction and windage losses. Input: mechanical ω (rad/s). Output: dissipated power (W).
node.ThermalMass.description: Lumped thermal capacitance (single-node RC). Input: power P_in (W). Internal state: temperature T (K).
node.ThermalNode.description: Node of a lumped thermal circuit. Four input ports (any may be left unconnected) to couple powers and conduction.
node.ThermalResistance.description: Linear thermal resistance (conduction/convection) between two nodes. Inputs: T_hot, T_cold (K). Output: heat flow Q (W).
node.CoolingSystem.description: Set of cooling-system parameters tunable live (ambient temperature, capacity, efficiency).
node.ConvectiveCooling.description: Active cooling: extracts heat from a hot node toward a cold side (typically the ambient output of a CoolingSystem).
node.DesignTemplate.description: Set of machine design requirements: target torque, speed, bus voltage and cooling class. Each requirement appears on an output port.
node.PMSMSizing.description: Classical analytical sizing of a surface PMSM. Given the target torque T and speed ω, computes the rotor diameter D and length L.
node.IPMSizing.description: Sizing of an IPM (interior permanent magnet) machine. Same approach as PMSMSizing with a reluctance boost factor.
node.BLDCSizing.description: BLDC sizing (trapezoidal commutation). Same closed-form bore/stack formula as PMSMSizing with a trapezoidal waveform correction.
node.PMSMElectromagnetic.description: Lumped-parameter electromagnetic model for a surface PMSM. Inputs: D (m), L (m). Computes torque, flux, inductances.
node.AirgapFluxDensity.description: Instantaneous radial flux density in the airgap, observed at a fixed point of the stator. Input: mechanical ω (rad/s).
node.PMSMEfficiency.description: Analytical efficiency of a surface PMSM. Inputs: torque T (Nm), ω (rad/s), Ke. Output: efficiency η ∈ [0,1].
node.DegToRad.label: Deg → Rad
node.DegToRad.description: Converts an angle in degrees to radians: out = in · π/180.
node.RadToDeg.label: Rad → Deg
node.RadToDeg.description: Converts an angle in radians to degrees: out = in · 180/π.
node.Alias.label: Alias
node.Alias.description: Virtual reference to another node: emits the same signal as the target without needing a visible wire. Useful to avoid wire crossings in dense graphs.
node.Object3D.label: 3D Object
node.Object3D.description: References an object imported from the project catalog. The output is an untransformed geometry, ready to be plugged into a Transform Object or straight into the Scene Output.
node.SceneOutput.label: Scene Output
node.SceneOutput.description: Sink of the Geometry subgraph: groups what is rendered in the View3D panel. Accepts multiple geometry inputs that the renderer composes into a single scene.
node.TransformObject.label: Transform Object
node.TransformObject.description: Bridge between the signal graph and the scene subgraph: takes a geometry and modulates it with three vec(3) vectors — rotation (Euler XYZ, rad), translation (m), scale (dimensionless). Use Combine XYZ to build the vec(3) from three scalar signals.
node.Vec3Constant.label: Vec3 Constant
node.Vec3Constant.description: Editable vec(3) constant. Useful as a TransformObject input when rotation / translation / scale is fixed.
node.CombineXYZ.label: Combine XYZ
node.CombineXYZ.description: Packs three scalar signals into a vec(3). Signal → Vec3 bridge.
node.SeparateXYZ.label: Separate XYZ
node.SeparateXYZ.description: Unpacks a vec(3) into its three scalar components. Vec3 → Signal bridge.
node.VectorAdd.label: Vector Add
node.VectorAdd.description: Component-wise vector addition: out = a + b.
node.VectorSub.label: Vector Sub
node.VectorSub.description: Component-wise vector subtraction: out = a − b.
node.VectorScale.label: Vector Scale
node.VectorScale.description: Scalar product: out = k · v. The scalar k enters through the second port.
node.VectorDot.label: Vector Dot
node.VectorDot.description: Dot product: out = a · b = aₓ·bₓ + aᵧ·bᵧ + a_z·b_z. Returns a scalar.
node.VectorCross.label: Vector Cross
node.VectorCross.description: Cross product: out = a × b. Perpendicular to a and b.
node.VectorLength.label: Length
node.VectorLength.description: Euclidean norm: out = √(vₓ² + vᵧ² + v_z²). Returns a scalar.
node.VectorNormalize.label: Normalize
node.VectorNormalize.description: Unit vector in the direction of v: out = v / |v|. If v is the zero vector, stays at zero.
inspector.device_geometry_hint: To assign geometry to this device use the scene sub-graph: File → Import 3D Model, then Object3D + Transform Object + Scene Output.
popup.comment_label: Comment  (Ctrl+hover the node to see it)

Add a node like DCMotorModel to see it here.

Auto-scales Y to the average of the latest values
+ resumes following the last sample
